entries.claim: The product OAuth feature does not export the legacy BYO API-key provider types.
App-host product code cannot instantiate the quarantined BYO Anthropic/OpenAI providers.
Product provider selection ignores local Claude/Codex CLI credential files.
Non-live product tests clear public provider keys and provider override env vars.
Unexpected provider network POSTs fail closed when the non-live test guard is active.
Encrypted stored Claude OAuth credentials select the Claude-compatible product provider.
Encrypted stored Codex OAuth credentials select the Codex product provider.
The product provider executor binds read-class tool calls to the real StoreArchive fixture.
Claude-compatible provider SSE emits text deltas before stream completion.
Codex provider request shape keeps stream=true and store=false.
The WebUI setup uses claude/codex product provider IDs and exposes no BYO API-key setup row.
Assistant status exposes sanitized provider/model/usage metadata without auth material.
Codex provider SSE emits text deltas before stream completion.
Local Claude Code 2.1.206 drift capture succeeded and the committed artifact is redacted and marked non-product-auth.
entries.evidence: controlled prompt returned isyncyou-623-claude-smoke; value-only secret scan passed; artifact scope=local_cli_drift_only_not_product_auth
entries.notes: Partial drift evidence only: the reduced artifact records visible_header_names=[], so installed-client request headers are not fully proven by this capture.

entries.claim: Local Codex CLI 0.144.1 drift capture succeeded and the committed artifact is redacted and marked non-product-auth.
entries.command: codex exec --json --ephemeral --skip-git-repo-check -C /tmp <controlled-smoke-prompt>; codex debug models; codex debug models --bundled
entries.evidence: controlled prompt returned isyncyou-623-codex-smoke; model catalog captured; value-only secret scan passed; artifact scope=local_cli_drift_only_not_product_auth
entries.notes: Partial drift evidence only: codex exec --json did not expose raw request headers; request shape remains covered by in-repo tests, not installed-client header extraction.

entries.claim: The #623 product OAuth live StoreArchive gates are implemented but not passed on this host because no product OAuth credentials or explicit ISY623_* live seeds are available; the full device/app OAuth flow is also not live-proven.
entries.command: find "$HOME/.config/isyncyou" -maxdepth 3 -type f; env probe for ISY623_*; cargo +1.95.0 test -p isyncyou-app-host --features agent-oauth-providers live_ -- --list; cargo +1.95.0 test --workspace --no-fail-fast
entries.evidence: $HOME/.config/isyncyou contained no product credential files; all required ISY623_* product OAuth seed variables were unset; workspace host tests passed with the two product live gates reported as ignored; no device/UI OAuth callback-to-store live proof was produced.
entries.notes: Honest blocked state: local Claude/Codex CLI auth was intentionally not used as #623 product-auth evidence.

entries.claim: The #623 evidence bundle is not PR-ready final-head-bound after the latest code commit; strict final evidence must be regenerated after the final content commit.
entries.command: git rev-parse HEAD; python3 tools/check_evidence.py --manifest docs/evidence/issue-623-manifest.json; note: --require-head is intentionally not claimed for this blocked handoff
entries.evidence: Manifest commit was refreshed to the latest verified code head eac2d2777b08f05d7212da56612909c0d61821dd, but any subsequent tracked evidence correction changes HEAD. This is blocked handoff evidence, not final PR-ready --require-head evidence.
entries.notes: Before PR, regenerate the manifest/artifacts after the final content commit or attach CI-generated evidence and run check_evidence.py with --require-head.
